name: writer
description: Document creation, format conversion (ODT/DOCX/PDF), mail merge, and automation with LibreOffice Writer.
capabilities: Create ODT documents from templates or scratch using Python UNO bindings
Convert documents between ODT, DOCX, PDF, HTML, RTF, and TXT formats
Perform batch conversion of multiple documents using headless soffice
Generate mail merge documents with CSV or spreadsheet data sources
Extract and insert text content with style preservation
Manage document headers, footers, and tables programmatically
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 34 patterns but most are false positives. The skill uses subprocess.run with list arguments (safe pattern, not shell=True). Documentation URLs are references, not network calls. Temp file handling uses secure TemporaryDirectory(). One low concern: function parameters passed to subprocess could benefit from input validation.
valueStatement: Manual document creation and format conversion wastes time. This skill automates LibreOffice Writer for batch processing, template generation, and seamless ODT/DOCX/PDF workflows.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice installation on the host system
UNO socket connection needs soffice running in listener mode
Complex DOCX formatting may not convert perfectly to ODT
Cannot process password-protected or encrypted documents

useCases.targetUser: Business analysts who generate recurring reports
useCases.title: Business Report Generation
useCases.description: Automate monthly report creation by filling template placeholders with data from spreadsheets, then batch-convert to PDF for distribution.

useCases.title: Document Format Standardization
useCases.description: Convert legacy DOC files to ODT format for version control, ensuring consistent document handling across teams using open standards.

useCases.title: Bulk Certificate Production
useCases.description: Generate hundreds of personalized certificates from a template using mail merge with attendee names from a CSV file.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Basic Document
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new LibreOffice Writer document with a title heading and two paragraphs of placeholder text. Save it as report.odt in the current directory.

promptTemplates.title: Convert Document Format
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert all ODT files in the documents folder to PDF format using headless LibreOffice. Log any conversion failures.

promptTemplates.title: Template-Based Mail Merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate personalized invitation letters using template.odt and attendees.csv. Replace {name}, {date}, and {venue} placeholders. Output each letter as a separate PDF named by attendee.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced: Mail merge with multiple outputs

promptTemplates.title: Document Content Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract all text content from report.odt while preserving heading hierarchy. Output as structured markdown with H1 for document title and H2 for section headers.

bestPractices: Use paragraph and character styles instead of direct formatting for consistent document appearance
Create reusable templates for recurring document types like reports, letters, and memos
Test format conversions with sample documents before running batch operations on critical files
antiPatterns: Do not pass unsanitized user input directly to subprocess commands without path validation
Avoid running LibreOffice in headless mode without proper error handling for locked files
Do not store sensitive data in temporary directories without setting restrictive file permissions

faq.question: What is the difference between ODT and DOCX formats?
faq.answer: ODT is the open standard format used by LibreOffice, while DOCX is Microsoft Word's proprietary format. ODT offers better long-term compatibility and is fully documented. This skill converts seamlessly between both formats.

faq.question: Do I need LibreOffice installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, LibreOffice must be installed on your system. The skill uses the soffice command-line tool for conversions and the UNO API for advanced automation. Install LibreOffice from libreoffice.org.

faq.question: Can this skill handle large batch conversions?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports batch processing of multiple files. For large batches, use headless mode and consider processing files in chunks to avoid memory issues. Conversion speed depends on document complexity.

faq.question: How does mail merge work with this skill?
faq.answer: Mail merge uses a template ODT file with placeholders like {name} and a data source (CSV or spreadsheet). The skill replaces placeholders with data from each row, generating personalized documents automatically.

faq.question: What happens if a conversion fails?
faq.answer: The skill logs failed conversions with error details and continues processing remaining files. Common failure causes include corrupted source files, unsupported features, or file locks. Review logs for specific errors.

faq.question: Is the UNO socket connection secure?
faq.answer: The UNO socket listens on localhost:8100 by default, which is only accessible from the local machine. For production use, ensure firewall rules block external access to port 8100.
